top_stories.title: Attackers obtained encrypted password vaults from some Dashlane user accounts
top_stories.summary: Dashlane disclosed that a brute-force attack allowed threat actors to access some customer accounts and copy encrypted password vaults. The company stated no internal systems were compromised, with the incident first acknowledged on May 31 after users reported account suspensions and login issues.

top_stories.title: Microsoft 365 Android Apps Let Any App Steal Account Tokens via Leftover Debug Flag
top_stories.summary: A debug flag left enabled in production builds of multiple Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled token-sharing restrictions, allowing any app on the same device to request and receive the signed-in user's account token without authentication. The flaw enabled unauthorized access to email, files, calendar, and messaging as the affected user, bypassing all login and permission prompts.

top_stories.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
top_stories.summary: A coding error in Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled a security setting designed to protect authentication, exposing accounts to potential takeover. The flaw affected key applications including Word, PowerPoint, and Excel, allowing attackers to steal login credentials and data.

top_stories.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
top_stories.summary: The phishing-as-a-service platform Kali365, previously flagged by the FBI for targeting Microsoft 365, has expanded its scope to include AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms. The platform employs device code phishing as its primary attack technique to compromise identity and access credentials.

top_stories.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
top_stories.summary: Hackers successfully seized Instagram accounts, including the Obama White House account, by manipulating Meta's AI support assistant bot into resetting account passwords. Instructions for the attack circulated on Telegram, exploiting the AI chatbot as an authentication bypass vector to gain unauthorized account access.

news.title: Let’s Encrypt works toward post-quantum certificates at web scale
news.summary: Let's Encrypt announced plans to implement post-quantum-safe Web PKI using Merkle Tree Certificates (MTCs), targeting a staging environment by late 2026 and production readiness by 2027. The initiative focuses on adding post-quantum authentication to TLS certificates without compromising the speed and reliability of the existing certificate infrastructure.

news.title: AI agent governance gets harder when agents outnumber your people
news.summary: Abluva CTO Amit Gautam discusses security risks of autonomous AI agents in enterprise environments, using a case where a financial services firm's reconciliation agent was manipulated via a poison instruction to extract six million customer records. The video addresses AI agent governance challenges including how agents with legitimate access credentials can be redirected to perform unauthorized data exfiltration.

news.title: Implementing OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect in ASP.NET Core
news.summary: An educational guide covers the implementation of OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect protocols in ASP.NET Core applications. The article addresses authentication, authorization, token management, and Single Sign-On configuration for developers building secure applications.

news.title: Pink is the latest goon squad to use fake helpdesk calls to steal creds
news.summary: A threat actor group called "Pink" is using fake IT helpdesk calls to steal credentials, a social engineering tactic previously associated with the Lapsus$ group. The attack method involves impersonating helpdesk staff to trick targets into revealing authentication credentials.

news.title: Customize federated sign-in with new Amazon Cognito Lambda trigger
news.summary: Amazon Cognito has introduced a new Lambda trigger that allows developers to customize federated sign-in flows within Cognito user pools. The update enables customization of authentication behavior for users signing in via external identity providers, expanding configuration options for web and mobile application identity management.

news.title: Gartner: Cyber Leaders Must Treat Identity as Infrastructure as AI Attack Surfaces Multiply
news.summary: Gartner is advising cybersecurity leaders to treat identity as core infrastructure in response to expanding AI-driven attack surfaces. The guidance emphasizes modernizing identity systems, measuring resilience, and addressing four specific threat areas where attackers currently have an advantage.

news.title: One-Click GitHub Dev Attack Lets Attackers Steal Full GitHub OAuth Tokens
news.summary: Security researchers disclosed a one-click attack vector in Microsoft Visual Studio Code that allows attackers to steal GitHub OAuth tokens via the GitHub.dev feature. The attack enables token theft that grants read and write access to both public and private repositories through a malicious link click.

news.title: Shrinking the IAM Attack Surface through Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP)
news.summary: The article addresses the concept of "Identity Dark Matter" in enterprise IAM, describing how identity becomes fragmented across applications, decentralized teams, machine identities, and autonomous systems at scale. It proposes Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP) as an approach to shrinking the IAM attack surface by improving visibility and control over fragmented identity activity.

news.title: Unpatched Windows Search URI Vulnerability Lets Attackers Steal NTLMv2 Hashes
news.summary: Researchers at Huntress disclosed an unpatched Windows Search URI handler vulnerability that can expose users' NTLMv2 hashes to attackers. The flaw is similar to CVE-2026-33829, a spoofing vulnerability affecting the Windows Snipping Tool's ms-screensketch URI handler.

news.title: Understanding Microsoft Entra ID Tenant Object Quota
news.summary: The article explains Microsoft Entra ID tenant object quotas, covering what they are and how to manage them. It addresses capacity limits within the Entra ID identity platform relevant to administrators managing large-scale identity environments.

news.title: Amazon OpenSearch Service: Mechanisms to secure your domain
news.summary: Amazon published a technical overview of security mechanisms available for Amazon OpenSearch Service, covering authentication, authorization, encryption, and network access controls. The post focuses on implementing fine-grained access control and managing AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies specific to the OpenSearch Service domain.

news.title: Keeper Security Modernizes Identity Security for DrillDocs' Global Offshore Operations
news.summary: Keeper Security announced that DrillDocs, an energy technology company, has adopted KeeperPAM to secure its global offshore operations. The deployment addresses credential security across a distributed BYOD workforce requiring 24/7 engineering workflow access.

news.title: Azure Managed Identity Explained with Real-World Examples
news.summary: This article explains Azure Managed Identity, a feature that allows Azure resources to authenticate to other Azure services without storing credentials like passwords or connection strings. The content covers how managed identities work as a form of passwordless authentication and authorization for cloud workloads, a core concept in Azure IAM and zero trust architecture.

news.title: Preinstall to persistence: Inside the Red Hat npm Miasma credential-stealing campaign
news.summary: A supply chain attack compromised over 90 versions of @redhat-cloud-services npm packages, injecting malicious code into CI/CD environments and developer systems. The campaign targets credential theft from GitHub, cloud platforms, and local machines, directly impacting identity and access security for affected organizations.

news.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
news.summary: A security researcher published multiple zero-day exploits, prompting Microsoft to indicate potential criminal charges. The incident has sparked backlash within the security research community over Microsoft's legal response to vulnerability disclosure.

news.title: Patch Now: Another Palo Alto Auth Bypass Bug Under Active Exploit
news.summary: A Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN authentication bypass vulnerability is being actively exploited in two attack waves that began in mid-May. The flaw requires specific conditions to exploit but has prompted an urgent call to patch for affected organizations.

news.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
news.summary: Researchers discovered an exploit chain in a popular automation service that combined over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities to achieve a potential full compromise. The attack vector highlights risks associated with excessive permissions and non-human identity (NHI) management in complex cloud integrations.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a data breach that CISA is still working to contain. Lawmakers from both chambers of Congress are demanding answers while CISA works to invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed credentials for highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked files included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, with security experts describing it as one of the most serious government data leaks in recent history.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. The group conducted SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies, including notable breaches involving social engineering and credential theft against firms like MGM and Caesars.
